name: Animated Tooltip
summary: An interactive tooltip component that follows cursor movement with smooth animations, revealing contextual information, images, or rich content on hover.
detailedDescription: The Animated Tooltip component elevates traditional hover tooltips into an engaging, dynamic interaction that reveals contextual information with fluid motion. Unlike static tooltips that simply appear, this component tracks cursor movement and animates into position, creating a more polished and intentional feel. The tooltip can display various content types including text, images, icons, or even complex nested components, making it versatile for different use cases. The signature feature is its mouse-following behavior - the tooltip position updates smoothly as the cursor moves, with configurable offset distances to prevent overlap with the trigger element. Animation typically combines fade-in effects with subtle scale or slide transforms for a natural appearance. The component includes intelligent positioning logic that detects viewport edges and adjusts placement to keep tooltips fully visible. Perfect for providing additional context, showing preview content, explaining features, or revealing supplementary information without cluttering the interface. The component respects accessibility standards by supporting keyboard focus states and screen reader announcements, ensuring the information is available to all users regardless of interaction method.
purpose: Provide contextual information and explanations without permanent interface clutter
Reveal additional details, previews, or supplementary content on demand
Enhance UX by making help text and explanations available exactly when needed
Create engaging micro-interactions that feel responsive and polished
Support both simple text tooltips and rich content with images or components
behavior: Appears smoothly on hover or focus with configurable animation timing
Follows cursor movement with smooth position updates and easing
Intelligently detects viewport edges and adjusts placement to remain visible
Supports multiple positioning options (top, bottom, left, right, auto)
Maintains visibility while cursor remains on trigger or tooltip itself
Dismisses smoothly when cursor exits both trigger and tooltip areas
Supports keyboard navigation and screen reader accessibility
visualCharacteristics: Smooth, fluid animations that feel responsive and intentional
Clean, readable design with appropriate contrast and typography
Flexible content area supporting text, images, and complex layouts
Subtle shadows or borders for clear visual separation from background
Configurable styling to match brand identity and design system
Responsive positioning that adapts to available viewport space
recommendations: Use where moderate motion and reusable composition improve UX and clarity.
